name: Cairnhold
descriptionSeed: The Ashwardens' city, and the least frightening place in the world once you are inside it. Cairnhold is built along the cairn-lines — low grey terraces following the stone rows out onto the heath — and every house has a second door that opens toward the fields, because that is the door the dead leave by and nobody here considers this morbid. The market sells bread and rope and good boots. Children run in the lanes. People come to the Palelands expecting a silence and find a town that is simply very calm and very unhurried, where the one thing nobody will lie to you about is how long something has left. A waygate stands at the north cairn, and the Ashwardens keep a bench beside it, because a great many people arrive here needing to sit down first.
encounterFlavor: Ashwardens who are kind, unhurried, and entirely at ease with the subject; families who came to visit someone and stayed a season; a stonecutter who will ask, gently and only once, whether you want your own cairn sited while you are here to choose the spot.
questSeeds: Someone's cairn has been cut with the wrong date and the family will not say which of them did it
A visitor has been in Cairnhold four months, 'about to leave', and the Ashwardens will not push but would like someone to ask
The north cairn-line is being extended toward the Quickwood and the Rootkin have Opinions
worldPosNote: The Palelands' haven — Ashwarden bearing, pulled inward because it is a place people live. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits. Independent of axisVector, which is WHAT it is; a bastion is where the two disagree.)
